raw.objective: To provide a PK model that combines CBZ and CBZE and incorporates inter- and intra-variability.
raw.covariates: Tested: Included: Dose, WT, VPA, PHT, PHB on CL of CBZ Dose, WT, VPA, PHT on CL of CBZE
raw.observations: Ka CBZ fixed to lit values. Vd/F of CBZ and CBZE fixed to literature values. Clearance of CBZ used as input for 1 compartment model of CBZE. Much variability explained by covariate model, allowing for more individualised dosing. The IIV on CBZE CL is very large, which does not allow for dose optimization regarding CBZE exposure, but does allow this for CBZ, due to large portion of variability explained.
raw.structure: 2 * 1 compartment, linear absorption and elimination separately done for CBZ and CBZE
raw.equations: [CBZ] CL/F=theta1*(Dose**theta2)*(WT**theta3) *(theta4**VPA) *(theta5**PHT) *(theta6**PHB)*exp(eta1) Vd/F=theta7*exp(eta2) Ka=theta8 Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1)+eps2 [CBZE] CL/F=theta1*(Dose**theta2)*(WT**theta3) *(theta4**VPA) *(theta5**PHT)+eta1 Vd/F=theta6 Ka was set to individuals’ elimination rates of CBZ (CL/Vd), with transformation ratio of 0.65, i.e. 65% of CBZ in blood is eventually converted to CBZE Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) VPA=comedication with valproic acid >18 mg/kg/day (yes =1, no=0) PHT=comedication with phenytoin (yes =1, no=0) PHB=comedication with phenobarbital (yes =1, no=0)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.141 theta2=0.406 theta3=0.117 theta4=1.23 theta5=1.44 [CBZ] theta6=1.26 theta7=72 theta8=1.2 omega1=0.0106 omega2=0.184 sigma1=0.0209 sigma2=0.454 [CBZE] theta6=175 omega1=22.6 sigma1=0.0875

extraction_target.title: Population pharmacokinetic modeling of steady state clearance of carbamazepine and its epoxide metabolite from sparse routine clinical data.

study.objective: To provide a PK model that combines CBZ and CBZE and incorporates inter- and intra-variability.

included: PHB on CL of CBZ Dose
covariates.raw: Tested: Included: Dose, WT, VPA, PHT, PHB on CL of CBZ Dose, WT, VPA, PHT on CL of CBZE

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
